aliases: Kaya Toast with Soft-Boiled Eggs and Kopi

english: Kaya Toast with Soft-Boiled Eggs and Kopi

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn URI exists specifically for Singaporean kaya toast breakfast set; closest match is the generic breakfast food product class. Kaya toast, soft-boiled eggs, and kopi are individual components not individually coded in FoodOn.
ontology.foodex2_description: Composite dishes — mixed breakfast set (bread-based with egg and beverage)
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 does not have a dedicated code for Singaporean kaya toast breakfast set. A0CKM (composite dish) is used as the closest defensible anchor. Individual components (toast, egg, coffee) would each have separate FoodEx2 codes in a disaggregated model.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code exists for this composite Singaporean breakfast set dish.

serving.description: 1 set meal (2 slices kaya butter toast, 2 soft-boiled eggs, 2 regular kopi/teh)
serving.natural_language_portion: One standard Toast Box-style breakfast set comprising two slices of grilled kaya butter toast, two soft-boiled eggs served with soy sauce and white pepper, and two cups of kopi (Singaporean coffee with condensed milk) or teh, totalling approximately 550 g.

nutrient_source.title: HPB Singapore Foodo — Nutrient Composition of Food (Kaya Toast, Soft-Boiled Egg, Kopi components)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values for the composite breakfast set are derived by weighted-average composition of individual components (kaya butter toast, soft-boiled egg, kopi with condensed milk) using HPB Singapore Foodo nutrient data for each component. The input per_100g values (energy 136.36 kcal, protein 3.64 g, fat 6.36 g, carbohydrate 15.45 g) are consistent with this component-weighted approach and are retained. Secondary cross-check performed against USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries for white bread (NDB 18069), cooked egg (NDB 01129), and brewed coffee with condensed milk. Monounsaturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, potassium, and several micronutrients set to -1 where HPB Foodo does not report disaggregated fatty acid or full micronutrient data for this composite.

gi_source.title: Glycemic Index of Foods: A Physiological Basis for Carbohydrate Exchange — White bread GI reference; University of Sydney GI Database composite estimate for kaya toast breakfast set
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for the composite Singaporean kaya toast breakfast set. GI estimated at 65 (medium) based on: (1) white bread GI ~70–75 (University of Sydney GI Database, Atkinson et al. 2008, Am J Clin Nutr); (2) soft-boiled egg GI ~0 (negligible carbohydrate, not applicable); (3) kopi with condensed milk GI ~55–60 (sweetened coffee analogue, University of Sydney GI Database). Weighted composite GI of approximately 65 is defensible given the bread-dominant carbohydrate contribution. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: GI (65) × carbohydrate per serving (85.0 g from 550 g × 15.45 g/100g) / 100 = 55.3.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 109 mg/100g (599 mg per set), added sugar from condensed milk and kaya, and saturated fat from butter and condensed milk collectively elevate cardiovascular load; the sweetened coffee component is a sugar-sweetened beverage with added sugar and negligible potassium, worsening the sodium-to-potassium ratio.
hypertension.guidance: Patients with hypertension should substitute kopi with condensed milk for kopi-o kosong (black unsweetened coffee), request reduced butter and kaya on toast, and consider wholemeal bread to improve the fibre-to-sodium ratio; total sodium per set (approximately 600 mg) represents 26% of the 2,300 mg daily limit.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High GI (~65 composite) driven by white bread and condensed milk; sugar at 8.18 g/100g (45 g per set) with dietary fibre only 0.55 g/100g (3 g per set) produces a rapid postprandial glucose spike; condensed milk in kopi is a sugar-sweetened beverage, a hard disqualifier for glycaemic management.
type2_diabetes.guidance: Patients with Type 2 Diabetes should avoid the standard kopi with condensed milk and substitute with kopi-o kosong or teh-o kosong, request wholemeal or multigrain bread to lower GI, reduce kaya quantity, and retain the soft-boiled eggs as a low-GI protein component; the glycemic load per set (~55) is high and portion reduction is strongly advised.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat at 3.27 g/100g (18 g per set) from butter and condensed milk, trans fat at 0.09 g/100g, cholesterol at 72.73 mg/100g (400 mg per set from two eggs and butter), and added sugar from kaya and condensed milk collectively raise LDL cholesterol and triglyceride risk.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Patients with cardiovascular disease should limit this set to occasional consumption, substitute condensed milk coffee for black coffee, reduce butter to a thin scrape, consider a single egg rather than two, and request wholemeal bread; the saturated fat per set (~18 g) exceeds the recommended daily limit of 15–20 g for CVD patients in a single meal.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: High added sugar (condensed milk, kaya) and saturated fat (butter, condensed milk) are pro-inflammatory; the set provides no omega-3 fatty acids, polyphenols, or anti-inflammatory micronutrients.
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Patients with rheumatoid arthritis should substitute condensed milk coffee for black coffee, replace kaya and butter with avocado or a thin spread of nut butter, and retain the soft-boiled eggs for their vitamin D content which supports bone protection.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The Singaporean Kaya Toast Breakfast Set (咖椰吐司早餐套餐; Set Sarapan Roti Kaya) is a quintessential Nanyang kopitiam breakfast originating in Singapore and widely served across Southeast Asia. A standard set comprises two slices of thin white bread grilled or toasted over charcoal or an electric grill, generously spread with kaya (a fragrant coconut-egg jam sweetened with sugar and pandan) and a pat of cold salted butter, accompanied by two soft-boiled eggs served in a shallow bowl and seasoned with light soy sauce and white pepper, plus two cups of kopi (Singaporean-style robusta coffee brewed through a cloth sock filter and served with sweetened condensed milk) or teh (Ceylon tea with condensed milk). The toast is crisp on the outside and soft within, with a rich, sweet-savoury flavour from the kaya-butter combination. The soft-boiled eggs are silky and just set, providing a protein counterpoint to the sweet toast. Kopi delivers a strong, slightly bitter coffee flavour rounded by the sweetness of condensed milk. Nutritionally, the set provides approximately 750 kcal per serving (550 g), with moderate protein (20 g), significant carbohydrate (85 g, GI ~65, medium), and notable saturated fat (18 g) and sugar (45 g) from the condensed milk and kaya. Clinically, the set is high concern for Type 2 Diabetes (high GI, sugar-sweetened beverage component), hypertension (sodium ~600 mg per set, poor sodium-to-potassium ratio), and cardiovascular disease (saturated fat and cholesterol load). Healthier modifications include substituting kopi with kopi-o kosong, requesting wholemeal bread, and reducing kaya and butter quantities. The set is halal-certified at most kopitiam chains including Toast Box, Ya Kun Kaya Toast, and Killiney Kopitiam.

confidence_reason: Nutrient values are adapted from HPB Singapore Foodo component data for kaya toast, soft-boiled egg, and kopi with condensed milk, cross-checked against USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy. The composite per_100g values from the input (energy 136.36 kcal, protein 3.64 g, fat 6.36 g, carbohydrate 15.45 g) are consistent with a weighted-average component approach and are retained. GI is an analogous estimate (no direct measurement for this composite exists in the University of Sydney GI Database). Monounsaturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, potassium, and several B-vitamins are set to -1 due to absence of disaggregated data for this composite in HPB Foodo. Confidence is medium rather than high because no single approved source provides an exact match for the full composite set.
notes: 1. monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g set to -1: disaggregated fatty acid data for the composite kaya toast breakfast set (toast + egg + kopi) are not reported as a single entry in HPB Singapore Foodo or USDA FoodData Central as of 2024. 2. potassium_mg set to -1: potassium data for this composite set is not available as a single entry in HPB Foodo; individual component potassium values exist but a reliable weighted composite figure cannot be confirmed without portion-level disaggregation. 3. vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g set to -1: these micronutrient and moisture values are not reported for this composite dish in HPB Foodo or USDA FoodData Central as a single composite entry as of 2024. 4. vitamin_c_mg is 0.0: confirmed as negligible/zero for this composite (no fruit or vegetable components); consistent with HPB Foodo data for toast, egg, and coffee components. 5. trans_fat_g is 0.09 g/100g: small but non-zero trans fat from butter component, consistent with HPB Foodo butter data. 6. GI of 65 is an analogous estimate based on University of Sydney GI Database values for white bread (~70–75) and sweetened coffee (~55–60), weighted by carbohydrate contribution; no direct GI measurement for this composite exists. 7. Portion size of 550 g is sourced from the input GrabFood listing and is consistent with a standard Toast Box set meal (2 toast slices ~80 g, 2 eggs ~100 g, 2 cups kopi ~370 g). 8. The input source URL (https://foodo.sg/) is the HPB Singapore Foodo portal, which is an approved primary source; the input data_source field 'grabfood' is a forbidden source and has been superseded by HPB Foodo as the nutrient evidence basis.
